name: Kustomer API
what_it_does: Kustomer's REST API for CRM-native customer service, combining full customer history, conversation management, and omnichannel support into a unified platform owned by Meta.
use_cases: Querying complete customer timeline data (purchases, interactions, events) for AI-driven support context
Creating and routing conversations with full CRM context attached from the first message
Building custom objects and data models to store domain-specific customer data for agents
Automating workflow triggers and business rules via API when conversation state changes
Syncing customer data between Kustomer and external CRMs or data warehouses
not_for: Small teams or startups due to enterprise-focused pricing and complexity
Simple ticket-only workflows that don't need full CRM customer timeline
Teams needing self-hosted or open-source customer support infrastructure
best_when: An agent needs rich customer 360 context (purchase history, prior interactions, custom attributes) directly within support conversations for high-touch customer service.
avoid_when: Your team only needs basic ticket management without deep CRM integration, or budget constraints make enterprise pricing prohibitive.

auth.notes: API keys generated per organization with bearer token authentication. Keys are org-scoped without fine-grained permission scopes. Separate keys can be created per integration for isolation purposes.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing only — starts around $89/user/month; custom contracts required
pricing.notes: Enterprise-only platform acquired by Meta in 2020. Pricing is negotiated per contract. No self-serve signup. Primarily targets mid-market and enterprise brands with high support volume.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; enterprise contracts include rate limit discussions

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses use standard JSON envelope with errors array and code/message fields. Error quality is generally good but some custom object validation errors are vague.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No native idempotency keys. Duplicate API calls will create duplicate resources. Agents should implement deduplication using search before creation.
known_agent_gotchas: Kustomer's data model distinguishes Customers, Conversations, and Messages — must understand the hierarchy before building any integration
Custom objects require schema creation before data can be written — cannot write to a custom object that doesn't exist in the org schema
Rate limits are not publicly documented and vary by contract — build conservative throttling into agents from the start
Webhook delivery is not guaranteed to be in order — agents relying on event sequence must handle out-of-order delivery
Meta acquisition means roadmap and API changes may be unpredictable — monitor changelog closely
Org-level API keys provide full access; no way to restrict a key to read-only or specific resources

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II, HIPAA, and PCI-DSS compliant. API keys lack fine-grained scopes. Enterprise contracts typically include security reviews and custom DPA agreements.
